svg.title: Valkyrja exception hierarchy — TypeScript
svg.desc: TypeScript-specific exception hierarchy for Valkyrja. All branches extend Error. Abstract via abstract class. No typed throws — Result pattern available as additive opt-in layer.
svg.text: No typed throws — all branches extend Error. abstract class prevents instantiation at compile time.
abstract class · always present · extends Valkyrja*
shared across Http · Cli · Queue etc.
concrete · one per throw site or group
All three branches extend Error — TypeScript has no distinct RuntimeError or InvalidArgumentError built-ins.
TypeScript cannot express thrown types on function signatures. Hierarchy is enforced structurally via abstract class, not by the compiler.
abstract class prevents new ValkyrjaThrowable() at compile time — same guarantee as PHP/Java/Python abstract, different mechanism.
tryMake<T> style Result<T, E> wrappers available as opt-in alongside standard throw/catch. Not required — additive layer only.
Decorators annotate exceptions for cache metadata at build time only — not used as structural wiring in the exception hierarchy.
